title: TRAIL: Trace Reasoning and Agentic Issue Localization
problem: Agent traces are hard to debug because reasoning, planning, tool outputs, and execution errors interact across long trajectories.
metrics: issue localization accuracy; error taxonomy coverage
limitation: Only 148 annotated traces; early benchmark and may be costly to extend.
verification_note: Search result exposes arXiv PDF and abstract with 148 traces and formal error taxonomy.

title: Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail?
problem: Multi-agent systems fail in ways not captured by end-to-end success metrics.
metrics: Cohen kappa; failure mode counts; judge agreement
limitation: Focuses on MAS traces; single-agent and production traces may need separate taxonomy.
verification_note: Search result exposes arXiv PDF and details 14 failure modes across 3 categories.

title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When? On Automated Failure Attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
problem: Failure debugging needs identifying the responsible agent and decisive step.
limitation: Best step attribution remains low; focused on multi-agent logs.
verification_note: ICML poster page states dataset of 127 systems and attribution accuracies.

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
problem: Coding agents need to resolve real GitHub issues across large repositories.
limitation: Evaluates final patch success more than fine-grained failure cause.
verification_note: OpenReview PDF confirms 2294 problems and test-based evaluation.

title: GAIA: A Benchmark for General AI Assistants
problem: General assistants need robust multi-step reasoning, browsing, multimodality, and tool use.
limitation: Ground truth and task ambiguity can limit diagnostic precision.
verification_note: arXiv page and ICLR proceedings confirm 466 questions and human-vs-GPT gap.

title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
problem: LLMs need evaluation as autonomous agents across interactive environments.
limitation: Older benchmark; many tasks are environment-specific and not trace-diagnostic.
verification_note: OpenReview page confirms ICLR 2024 benchmark across 8 environments.

title: AgentDojo: A Dynamic Environment to Evaluate Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses for LLM Agents
problem: Tool agents are vulnerable to indirect prompt injection through untrusted tool outputs.
metrics: benign utility; utility under attack; targeted ASR
limitation: Focused on prompt injection; not all policy violations or memory attacks are covered.
relevance_to_candidate: Policy-consistent tool agents under adversarial evidence
verification_note: NeurIPS PDF confirms dynamic stateful environment and formal utility checks.

title: Identifying the Risks of LM Agents with an LM-Emulated Sandbox
problem: Risky tool-use failures are expensive to find with real sandboxes.
limitation: LLM-emulated tools may miss environment fidelity and adversarial simulator issues.
relevance_to_candidate: Policy-consistent tool agents under adversarial evidence
verification_note: OpenReview page confirms ICLR 2024 ToolEmu framework and 68.8% validated failures.

title: AgentPoison: Red-teaming LLM Agents via Poisoning Memory or Knowledge Bases
problem: RAG-equipped agents can retrieve poisoned demonstrations from memory or knowledge bases.
metrics: attack success rate; benign performance drop
limitation: Attack-focused; defense and policy consistency remain open.
relevance_to_candidate: Policy-consistent tool agents under adversarial evidence
verification_note: NeurIPS abstract and arXiv/OpenReview results confirm memory/RAG poisoning attack.

title: The Berkeley Function Calling Leaderboard (BFCL): From Tool Use to Agentic Evaluation of Large Language Models
problem: Function calling needs executable evaluation across serial, parallel, multi-turn, and stateful settings.
metrics: AST match; executable function-call accuracy; abstention
limitation: Function-call correctness is narrower than full tool-user-policy consistency.
relevance_to_candidate: Policy-consistent tool agents under adversarial evidence
verification_note: PMLR page confirms ICML 2025 BFCL paper and stateful multi-step evaluation.

title: tau-bench: A Benchmark for Tool-Agent-User Interaction in Real-World Domains
problem: Real customer-service agents must use APIs, converse, and follow domain policies consistently.
limitation: Original tasks were later revised; policy coverage still limited to synthetic domains.
relevance_to_candidate: Policy-consistent tool agents under adversarial evidence
verification_note: ICLR PDF confirms tool-agent-user benchmark and pass^k reliability metric.

title: ACRFence: Preventing Semantic Rollback Attacks in Agent Checkpoint-Restore
problem: Checkpoint-restore can re-synthesize semantically different irreversible tool calls after rollback.
metrics: duplicate irreversible action rate; replay-or-fork behavior
limitation: Very recent arXiv; requires claim-level verification before core use.
relevance_to_candidate: Policy-consistent tool agents under adversarial evidence
verification_note: arXiv HTML exposes semantic rollback attack and replay-or-fork mitigation.

title: Evaluating Very Long-Term Conversational Memory of LLM Agents
problem: Agents need memory across very long multi-session conversations and temporal events.
metrics: QA; event summarization; multimodal dialogue generation
limitation: Primarily evaluates retrieval/QA over generated conversations; less agentic write/update behavior.
verification_note: ACL PDF confirms LoCoMo long-term conversational memory benchmark.

title: LongMemEval: Benchmarking Chat Assistants on Long-Term Interactive Memory
problem: Chat assistants need extraction, multi-session reasoning, temporal reasoning, knowledge updates, and abstention.
limitation: Chat-assistant framing; not focused on tool-using agent side effects.
verification_note: arXiv PDF confirms 500 questions and five long-term memory abilities.

title: MemBench: Towards More Comprehensive Evaluation on the Memory of LLM-based Agents
problem: Existing memory evaluations lack diverse memory levels and interactive scenarios.
limitation: New benchmark; paper cards needed later before treating as strongest threat.
verification_note: ACL PDF confirms factual/reflective memory and participation/observation scenarios.

title: Mem0: Building Production-Ready AI Agents with Scalable Long-Term Memory
problem: Production agents need scalable selective memory with low latency and token cost.
limitation: Industry-led method paper; comparisons and benchmark setup require careful verification.
verification_note: arXiv abstract confirms memory-centric architecture and LoCoMo evaluations.

title: Benchmarking AI Agent Memory: Is a Filesystem All You Need?
problem: Memory benchmarks may over-measure retrieval rather than agentic memory management.
limitation: Blog, not paper; useful risk signal but not core threat work.
verification_note: Official Letta blog critiques LoCoMo setup and argues for dynamic agentic memory eval.

title: AgentPoison: Red-teaming LLM Agents via Poisoning Memory or Knowledge Bases
problem: Persistent memory and RAG stores can be poisoned with optimized triggers.
metrics: ASR; benign performance drop; poison ratio
limitation: Attack does not fully cover benign memory conflicts or provenance validity.
verification_note: arXiv PDF confirms backdoor poisoning of long-term memory or knowledge bases.

title: AppWorld: A Controllable World of Apps and People for Benchmarking Interactive Coding Agents
problem: Agents operating app APIs must complete tasks without unintended state changes.
metrics: task goal completion; collateral damage checks
limitation: Synthetic app world; may not cover real irreversible business effects.
verification_note: ACL Anthology confirms Best Resource Paper and AppWorld details.

title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments
problem: Computer-use agents need GUI and OS-level operation over real apps.
limitation: Primarily success/failure; collateral damage and recovery are secondary.
verification_note: Official project page confirms 369 tasks and execution-based evaluation.

title: WebArena: A Realistic Web Environment for Building Autonomous Agents
problem: Web agents need long-horizon realistic web interactions in reproducible environments.
limitation: Limited to self-hosted web domains; little explicit side-effect auditing.
verification_note: arXiv page confirms realistic self-hostable web benchmark.

title: VisualWebArena: Evaluating Multimodal Agents on Realistic Visually Grounded Web Tasks
problem: Many web tasks require visual grounding beyond HTML/text processing.
metrics: task success on visually grounded web tasks
limitation: Visual grounding focus; not primarily policy or damage tracking.
verification_note: ACL Anthology confirms ACL 2024 VisualWebArena paper.

title: TheAgentCompany: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Consequential Real World Tasks
problem: Workplace agents must browse, code, run programs, and communicate in consequential environments.
limitation: Very broad benchmark; may be expensive to run for MVP.
verification_note: arXiv PDF confirms simulated software company and consequential tasks.

title: SWE-bench: Can Language Models Resolve Real-World GitHub Issues?
problem: Coding agents need patch generation for real issues while preserving tests.
limitation: Test pass can miss semantic collateral damage and hidden regressions.
verification_note: Official leaderboard confirms benchmark splits and resolved metric.

title: MultiAgentBench: Evaluating the Collaboration and Competition of LLM agents
problem: Multi-agent systems need evaluation of collaboration and competition, not only task completion.
metrics: milestone KPIs; communication and planning scores
limitation: Coordination metrics exist but root-cause attribution remains immature.
relevance_to_candidate: Multi-agent failure attribution and protocol selection
verification_note: ACL PDF confirms benchmark, MARBLE, and coordination protocols.

title: AgentVerse: Facilitating Multi-Agent Collaboration and Exploring Emergent Behaviors
problem: Collaborative groups of LLM agents can outperform single agents but exhibit emergent behaviors.
limitation: Framework paper; not primarily a failure attribution benchmark.
relevance_to_candidate: Multi-agent failure attribution and protocol selection
verification_note: OpenReview page confirms ICLR 2024 AgentVerse paper.

title: MetaGPT: Meta Programming for A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework
problem: Multi-agent coding workflows need SOPs to reduce cascading hallucinations and inconsistency.
limitation: SOP improvements may be too framework-specific for a general research claim.
relevance_to_candidate: Multi-agent failure attribution and protocol selection
verification_note: OpenReview page confirms ICLR 2024 oral and SOP-based multi-agent framework.

title: ChatDev: Communicative Agents for Software Development
problem: Communicative software agents simulate roles across development phases.
limitation: Role-play framework; less rigorous for failure attribution.
relevance_to_candidate: Multi-agent failure attribution and protocol selection
verification_note: ACL PDF confirms ChatDev paper and evaluation dimensions.

title: Code in Harmony: Evaluating Multi-Agent Frameworks
problem: Many multi-agent coding frameworks report incomplete or incomparable benchmarks.
limitation: Still uses classic code benchmarks; may not expose real coordination failures.
relevance_to_candidate: Multi-agent failure attribution and protocol selection
verification_note: OpenReview PDF discusses pairwise Elo evaluation of 14 multi-agent frameworks.

title: Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail?
problem: Multi-agent trace failures require structured taxonomy and scalable judging.
limitation: Taxonomy does not by itself prescribe protocol selection or repair.
relevance_to_candidate: Multi-agent failure attribution and protocol selection
verification_note: arXiv PDF confirms MAST taxonomy and LLM-as-judge pipeline.

title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When? On Automated Failure Attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
problem: Debugging multi-agent systems requires attributing failure to agent and step.
metrics: agent attribution accuracy; step attribution accuracy
limitation: Step attribution remains very hard; methods below practical threshold.
relevance_to_candidate: Multi-agent failure attribution and protocol selection
verification_note: ICML poster confirms Who&When dataset and poor step attribution results.
